title: dianthus orientalis subsp. ketzkhovelii
intro: Dianthus orientalis subsp. ketzkhovelii, also known as Ketzkhovel's pink, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the carnation family. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dianthus orientalis subsp. ketzkhovelii include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for its medicinal properties to be harnessed and utilized for various health benefits.
